subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Player-Count Audit
- **Supported counts** — 2 to 4 players.
- **Recommended** — Best with a consistent group of 4 across the entire campaign.
- **Solo mode** — Not officially supported.

#### Core Components
- **Game board** — Persistent world map that accumulates stickers, damage, and changes over the campaign.
- **Player cards** — City cards, event cards, and epidemic cards (some destroyed during play).
- **Infection cards** — City infection deck with permanent modifications possible.
- **Disease cubes** — 4 colors of disease cubes (standard Pandemic setup).
- **Research stations** — 6 research stations.
- **Character cards** — Role cards that persist between sessions with upgrades and scars.

#### Legacy Components
- **Sealed dossiers** — 8 numbered sealed boxes and dossiers opened when instructed.
- **Sticker sheets** — Multiple sticker sheets for permanent board modifications.
- **Legacy deck** — Cards revealed at specific game milestones.
- **Upgrade stickers** — Character upgrades, city upgrades, positive and negative mutations.
- **Scar stickers** — Permanent negative effects applied to characters.
- **Objective cards** — Monthly objectives that change throughout the campaign.

#### Important Note
- **Permanent changes** — Cards may be torn up, stickers permanently alter the board, and rules evolve. This is a one-time-play campaign.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Initial Setup (January, Game 1)
1. **Place board** — Set up the board as in standard Pandemic.
2. **Outbreak track** — Place marker on 0.
3. **Infection rate** — Place marker on the starting position.
4. **Cure markers** — Place 4 cure markers vial-side up.
5. **Atlanta station** — Place 1 research station in Atlanta.
6. **Seed infections** — Draw 9 infection cards (3/3/3 pattern: 3 cubes, 2 cubes, 1 cube).
7. **Legacy deck** — Place the Legacy deck nearby; do not look through it.
8. **Sealed components** — Keep all sealed dossiers and boxes closed until instructed.

#### Subsequent Months
- **Board state persists** — The board retains all stickers and modifications from previous games.
- **New objectives** — Draw new objective cards as directed.
- **Funding adjustment** — Adjust funding level based on previous month results.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Character Selection
- **Choose characters** — Each player selects a character. Characters persist across the campaign.
- **Apply upgrades** — Characters retain any upgrade stickers from previous sessions.
- **Check scars** — Characters carry any scar stickers that affect their abilities.
- **Relationships** — Characters can have relationship upgrades with other characters.

#### Starting Hands
--- 2 Players ---
- **Starting hand** — 4 cards each.

--- 3 Players ---
- **Starting hand** — 3 cards each.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Starting hand** — 2 cards each.

#### Funding Level
- **Win previous month** — Funding decreases (fewer funded event cards).
- **Lose previous month** — Funding increases (more funded event cards).
- **Catch-up mechanism** — Funding acts as a difficulty balancer across the campaign.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### Monthly Game Start
- **Check objectives** — Review this month's mandatory and optional objectives.
- **Prepare player deck** — Include epidemic cards based on difficulty and funding.
- **Legacy deck check** — Draw from the Legacy deck if instructed at the start of the month.
- **Begin play** — Start with the normal Pandemic turn structure.
- **Replay rule** — If you lose a month, you may replay it once. Win or lose the replay, move to the next month.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Turn Sequence (Same as Base Pandemic)
1. **Do actions** — Perform up to 4 actions.
2. **Draw player cards** — Draw 2 cards from the Player Deck.
3. **Resolve epidemics** — If an Epidemic card is drawn, resolve it immediately.
4. **Hand limit** — Discard down to 7 cards if over limit.
5. **Infect cities** — Draw infection cards equal to current infection rate and place cubes.

#### Legacy Interrupts
- **Legacy deck triggers** — Certain game events (epidemics, outbreaks, discoveries) trigger Legacy deck draws.
- **Dossier openings** — The Legacy deck may instruct you to open a sealed dossier or box.
- **Immediate resolution** — Legacy events are resolved immediately when triggered, then play continues.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Standard Actions (from Base Pandemic)
- **Drive/Ferry** — Move to an adjacent connected city.
- **Direct Flight** — Discard a city card to fly to that city.
- **Charter Flight** — Discard your current city's card to fly anywhere.
- **Shuttle Flight** — Move between two cities with research stations.
- **Build Research Station** — Discard current city card to build a station.
- **Treat Disease** — Remove 1 cube (all cubes if cured) from your city.
- **Share Knowledge** — Give/take the city card matching your current city with another player there.
- **Discover a Cure** — At a research station, discard 5 same-color cards.

#### Legacy Actions (Introduced During Campaign)
- **Search** — Search a city for supplies or information (introduced via Legacy deck).
- **Character upgrades** — Apply upgrade stickers to improve character abilities.
- **Build military bases** — Certain story events unlock new structure types.
- **Note** — Additional actions are revealed as the campaign progresses; specifics are spoilers.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Permanent Consequences
- **City damage** — When a city outbreaks, it may receive a permanent sticker increasing future difficulty.
- **Rioting** — Cities with enough damage stickers enter a state of unrest, then collapse.
- **Fallen cities** — Collapsed cities cannot have research stations and are harder to operate in.
- **Card destruction** — Certain events instruct you to permanently destroy (tear up) cards.

#### Character Persistence
- **Scars** — Characters who are in a city during an outbreak may receive a scar sticker.
- **Scar effects** — Scars impose permanent penalties (fewer actions, limited movement, etc.).
- **Death** — A character with too many scars is lost permanently.
- **Upgrades** — Between games, apply positive upgrades earned during play.
- **Relationships** — Two characters who end a game in the same city may gain a relationship bonus.

#### Evolving Rules
- **New diseases** — Additional disease types may emerge during the campaign.
- **Rule changes** — The Legacy deck adds, modifies, and removes rules as the story progresses.
- **Objectives shift** — Monthly objectives change to reflect the evolving narrative.

#### Funding System
- **Post-game adjustment** — Winning lowers funding; losing raises it.
- **Event cards** — Funding determines how many funded event cards are shuffled into the Player Deck.
- **Balance tool** — Ensures the campaign remains challenging but not impossible.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Monthly Win/Loss
- **Win condition** — Complete all mandatory objectives for that month.
- **Loss conditions** — Same as base Pandemic: 8 outbreaks, cube supply exhaustion, or player deck exhaustion.
- **Replay** — A lost month can be replayed once; then move on regardless.

#### Campaign Scoring (After Month 12)
- **Months won** — Score points for each month won during the campaign.
- **Funding level** — Lower final funding level scores more points (indicates consistent winning).
- **End-game bonus objectives** — Certain late-campaign objectives provide additional scoring.
- **Board state** — Points or penalties for the final state of the game board.

#### Campaign Result
- **Total score** — Sum all campaign scoring categories.
- **Rating** — The rulebook provides a rating scale to evaluate your team's overall performance.
- **No competitive element** — This is a fully cooperative campaign score.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
- **Protect key cities** — Cities with no damage stickers are valuable; prevent outbreaks in important hubs.
- **Prioritize mandatory objectives** — Optional objectives give bonuses but are not worth risking a loss.
- **Manage character health** — Avoid putting scarred characters in risky positions.
- **Plan for the long campaign** — Decisions in January affect December; think ahead.
- **Use funding wisely** — Event cards from funding can turn losing months into wins.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Ignoring city damage** — Letting cities accumulate damage stickers creates cascading problems in later months.
- **Spreading scars across all characters** — Concentrate risky assignments to protect your best characters.
- **Wasting upgrades** — Apply upgrades strategically to characters who will benefit most long-term.
- **Forgetting Legacy deck triggers** — Missing a trigger can cause confusion; check trigger conditions carefully.
- **Playing too cautiously** — Sometimes accepting a loss is better than risking permanent damage to the board.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Decision 1: Risk vs Preservation
- **Aggressive play** — Push for optional objectives to maximize campaign score.
- **Conservative play** — Focus on mandatory objectives to preserve board and character state.

#### Decision 2: Character Investment
- **Upgrade one character** — Create a powerhouse character with multiple upgrades.
- **Spread upgrades** — Distribute upgrades across the team for flexibility.

#### Decision 3: City Triage
- **Protect all cities** — Spread resources to prevent any city from falling.
- **Sacrifice periphery** — Accept damage to less critical cities to protect vital hubs.
